text.title: Risk Management Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real PM deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Register lists 30 risks with strategy `mitigate` and no observable trigger. Nothing fires; surprises arrive un-prepared.
antipattern.root-cause: Author defaulted strategy to `mitigate` to avoid scoring debates; trigger field treated as optional.
antipattern.fix: Reject rows lacking `trigger`. Force a one-line observable signal per risk; if none exists, the risk is accepted (with documented reasoning), not mitigated.

antipattern.symptom: Every P=M, every I=M. Register has no prioritisation signal; sponsors ignore it.

root-cause.source: Kahneman anchoring research; PMBoK calibration practice

antipattern.root-cause: Team not calibrated; scoring rationale not required; agent auto-fills Medium-Medium when uncertain.
antipattern.fix: Require a one-line rationale per P and per I citing the source artefact. Quarterly base-rate calibration with historical archive.

antipattern.symptom: Agent rewrites scores between runs with no diff; nobody knows when something changed.
antipattern.root-cause: Agent given write access to the register file instead of emitting a diff.
antipattern.fix: Agent MUST emit NEW/CHANGED_SCORE/CLOSED/STALE diffs only; humans review and apply.

antipattern.symptom: Register full of `vendor delay`, `scope creep`, `key person leaves` and nothing project-specific.
antipattern.root-cause: Agent run without project charter / WBS / SOW context; output reflects training-data priors.
antipattern.fix: Seed the agent with the charter, WBS, ADRs, vendor SOWs; require source citation per risk; reject ungrounded entries.
